mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of Feb 2007 reflects recent Fed tightening cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns. A 0.3 percentage point increase in UNRATE over 3 months triggers this negative signal.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% as of Feb 2007, near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer spending momentum and aggregate demand strength. Accelerating retail sales over the preceding 2 months signal robust demand that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following periods of accelerating RSAFS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) indicates manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side economic strength. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, it signals emerging supply constraints and stagflationary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite INDPRO deceleration, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer and construction demand. When both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously over 2 months, it signals broad-based demand weakness that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand-side fragility not visible in either series alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 2% over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1534K as of Jan 2007, RSAFS at 617.7B
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging demand deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 0.1% month-over-month while UNRATE remains stable or rises, it signals labor market softening that reduces earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.1% AND UNRATE >= prior month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147.19M as of Feb 2007, UNRATE at 5.2%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deceleration
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE stability are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains above 5%, it signals real rate compression that constrains equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 273.01 as of Feb 2007, FEDFUNDS at 5.25%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following periods of accelerating inflation and elevated rates are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects supply-side capacity and manufacturing momentum. In late-cycle expansions (identified by PAYEMS growth > 0.15% month-over-month), accelerating INDPRO signals overheating and rising inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional and reverses in early-cycle recovery.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.15%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.59 as of Feb 2007, PAYEMS at 147.19M
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following joint INDPRO acceleration and strong payroll growth are not significantly negative in late-cycle regimes (t-test p > 0.05), or mechanism direction reverses in early-cycle recovery without regime adjustment.
